hero.question: Will SPD win the most seats in the 2026 Rhineland-Palatinate parliamentary elections?
hero.prob_take: At 36.5%, traders are pricing the SPD as a clear underdog in Rhineland-Palatinate, implying the CDU/Greens coalition or a fragmented right holds commanding structural advantage going into 2026. The +15 point spike in 24 hours smells like a real polling shift or campaign event, but without seeing the catalyst, you're buying momentum into what remains a hostile electoral map for the left.

daily_take.headline: SPD surge in Rhineland-Palatinate signals coalition math shifting fast
daily_take.deck: SPD jumped 15 points to 36.5% overnight, flipping the race narrative and crushing the CDU's 63.5% chalk. The move screams late polling data or backroom coalition chatter breaking through. Smart money is asking whether this reflects real momentum or traders frontrunning a narrative that hasn't aged well yet. The CDU's -15.5 point slide looks like panic selling, not informed repricing.

sidebar.headline: CDU collapse at 63.5% feels like capitulation. Watch if this holds or if German voters still anchor on Merz.
sidebar.label: CDU win most seats: 63.5%

sidebar.headline: Vitality's 56% looks thin for favorites. Tournament favorites rarely price this tight without recent form collapse.
sidebar.label: Vitality wins Blast Rotterdam: 56.0%

sidebar.headline: Anduril's -22 point dump to 18% reeks of insider deal momentum. Check filing calendars before the IPO speculation dies entirely.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO before 2027: 18.0%
